beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and get ready for the day.
beats.reasoning: Jackson can't start the day without a swim, so this quick morning routine gets him ready to head to the pool. Low energy from yesterday (40/100) suggests a restorative swim is exactly what he needs.

beats.activity: Commute to Northgate Pool for early morning swim.
beats.reasoning: Short drive within Northgate; Jackson prioritizes his morning swim ritual to clear his head and recharge after yesterday's emotionally intense day at Pride.

beats.activity: Swim laps and relax in the pool.
beats.reasoning: An hour of swimming to center himself—this is his non-negotiable start to any day, especially after a low-energy evening yesterday.
Drive back home to shower and have breakfast before heading out for the day's activities.

beats.activity: Shower, breakfast, and relax at home.
beats.reasoning: Post-swim routine—a quiet morning to recharge and enjoy the overcast Saturday vibe. Jackson tends to plan days around mood, and he's starting to feel more energized.
Ballard is across town from Northgate; the overcast weather is perfect for outdoor market browsing, and Jackson values spontaneous community time over crowds.

beats.activity: Browse the Ballard Farmers Market, pick up fresh produce and chat with vendors.
beats.reasoning: Saturday morning farmers market fits his spontaneous nature and preference for low-key community time. He can gather ingredients for cooking or knitting projects later.

beats.activity: Lunch at a nearby Ballard cafe or casual spot.
beats.reasoning: Grab lunch in Ballard while still in the neighborhood; Jackson is a spontaneous planner and prefers intimate local spots over chains.

beats.activity: Commute to T-Mobile Park to catch the Mariners game.
beats.reasoning: The 1pm Mariners home game is happening today; Jackson is spontaneous and competitive by nature, so catching a game fits his Saturday mood. Travel from Ballard to SoDo is ~35–40 min via I-5.

beats.activity: Watch the Mariners home game at T-Mobile Park.
beats.reasoning: Spontaneous Saturday outing; the Mariners game timing (1pm start) aligns perfectly. Jackson's competitive nature makes him enjoy the energy of live sports.
Drive back to Northgate after the game; overcast weather means no evening outdoor plans, so heading home to relax.

diary: Started the day right with an early swim at Northgate Pool—nothing clears the head like those cold laps first thing. The water was perfect, quiet at that hour. Grabbed breakfast back home and felt energized enough to head down to Ballard Farmers Market. The overcast sky kept things cool, which was nice for wandering. Picked up some gorgeous heirloom tomatoes and fresh basil from this older couple who really know their stuff. Had a decent lunch at one of the cafes on Wallingford, then made my way to T-Mobile Park for the Mariners game. The crowd was lively—SoDo was packed—and even though the team didn't pull off a win, there was something good about being there, surrounded by all that energy. Left feeling a little tired but settled.
thought_process: Saturday felt like a day to recenter myself. After years in school counseling, I've learned that structure—even on weekends—keeps me grounded. The swim was non-negotiable; it's my anchor. The farmers market appealed to me because I wanted to be around people and life, but on my own terms. The Mariners game was partly about tradition, partly about being part of something bigger than myself. The overcast weather actually helped—no pressure to optimize the day, just move through it. My mood stayed even throughout, maybe because I wasn't fighting the pace or the weather. Everything felt purposeful without being rushed.
world_event_prompt: Overcast Saturday, high of 64F. Farmers markets in Ballard, Fremont, and the U-District in full swing. Mariners home game at T-Mobile Park at 1pm brings SoDo crowds.
